type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The emission of Hexachlorobenzene is a function of the waste composition treated by the incinerator, and operational uptime, and is highly variable.

The air emissions at the SHTC are dependent on the quantity and composition of the waste processed.  In regards to the quantity of waste received the amounts can be highly variable from year to year.  As an example the total waste processed per year between 2009 to 2014 was:  2,837 tonnes (2009), 6,368 tonnes (2010), 7,049 tonnes (2011), 16,408 tonnes (2012), 17,584 tonnes (2013), and 15,203 tonnes (2014).  The composition of the waste can change from year to year depending on the needs of the market place at any given time.  These combined factors yielded a high degree of variability in emissions over time.
